title: narcissus cantabricus subsp. hedraeanthus
intro: Narcissus cantabricus subsp. hedraeanthus, also known as Narcissus hedraeanthus, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the Amaryllidaceae family, characterized by its white or pink trumpet-shaped flowers and narrow leaves. It is used in traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine and homocholine, exhibiting potential anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Narcissus cantabricus subsp. hedraeanthus may include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve cognitive function, and provide relief from symptoms of anxiety and depression. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be dried and powdered for use in capsules or as a supplement.
